paras.text: Kuch din beet gaye. Baat thodi normal ho chuki thi, but {{A}} ko wo doori abhi bhi khatakti thi. Ek shaam, library band hone se pehle, dono akele reh gaye.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: {{B}}, ek baat kahoon? Us din ke baad se lagta hai kuch badal gaya hai humare beech.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (kitab band karte hue) Nahi aisi koi baat nahi... bas thoda time laga.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Sach kahoon, agar maine ye project join na kiya hota, aaj tumhe itni achhi tarah jaanta hi nahi. Iske liye grammar me kya use hoga? Type 3 jaisa lag raha hai, kyunki 'join na kiya hota' past ki baat hai.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (muskurate hue, thodi narm hoke) Achha observation, but poori tarah Type 3 nahi hai — dhyan do, doosra part 'jaanta hi nahi' — ye to ABHI, PRESENT ki baat hai, past ki nahi! Jab ek sentence me DO ALAG time zones mix hote hain — ek part past ka, ek part present ka — usko kehte hain Mixed Conditional.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Ohh, matlab 'if' clause past ka, but result present ka? Ye kaise likhenge poora sentence?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: If I hadn't joined this project, I wouldn't know you so well now — dekho, 'if' clause me 'HAD/HADN'T + V3' hai (past), but main clause me sirf 'WOULD/WOULDN'T + V1' hai, 'would have' nahi — kyunki result ABHI present me hai, poora past me nahi.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (dhyan se sunte hue) Achha, matlab agar 'would have' laga dete, to galat ho jaata?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Bilkul galat ho jaata! Agar tum bologe 'I wouldn't have known you' — ye pure Type 3 ban jaayega, matlab poori baat past me hi khatam ho gayi, present se koi connection nahi. But hum to abhi bhi ek dusre ko jaante hain — ye abhi tak chal raha hai, isliye 'wouldn't know' (present), 'wouldn't have known' nahi (pure past).

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Samajh gaya! Ek aur example do na, isi type ka, taaki pakka clear ho jaaye.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo, ek achha example — 'Agar usne foreign me padhai na ki hoti, uske paas ye vishwa-drishti (global perspective) nahi hoti.' Dekho, 'padhai na ki hoti' past hai, 'nahi hoti' present hai — 'If she hadn't studied abroad, she wouldn't have this global perspective.' Ye bhi Mixed Conditional hai, same pattern.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Achha ab ek doosra type bhi hoga na Mixed Conditional ka? Ya sirf ye ek hi variation hai?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Bahut achha sawaal! Haan, ek doosra variation bhi hai — bilkul ULTA. Jab ek PRESENT/GENERAL fact ke basis pe hum PAST ke baare me guess ya conclusion nikalte hain.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Matlab? Ek example do na, samajh nahi aaya.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo dekho — maan lo tumhe pata hai ki main hamesha exams me bahut serious rehti hoon, ye ek present/general fact hai mere baare me. Ab agar maine kal ek test miss kar diya, to tum bolte — 'Agar wo itni serious nahi hoti (present fact), usne kal test miss nahi kiya hota (past result).'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Ohh, to isko English me kaise banayenge?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: If she weren't so serious about exams, she wouldn't have missed that test by mistake — actually, being so serious, she must have had a strong reason. — dekho is baar 'if' clause me PRESENT/GENERAL fact hai ('weren't so serious' — present trait), but main clause me PAST result hai ('wouldn't have missed' — kyunki wo kaam past me hua).

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (sochte hue) Ruko, ye to pehle wale se ulta structure hai — pehle wale me 'if' clause past tha, result present tha. Isme 'if' clause present hai, result past hai?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Ekdum sahi pakda! Yehi Mixed Conditional ke do variations hain — Type A: PAST cause, PRESENT result (jaisa humare project wala example tha). Type B: PRESENT/GENERAL cause, PAST result (jaisa exam-serious wala example). Dono me time-zones mix hote hain, bas direction ulta hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Ye thoda tricky hai, ek aur simple example do Type B ka, roz ki zindagi se.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo, ekdum simple — 'Agar wo itna lazy nahi hota (uska general trait/nature — present), usne pichle saal exam fail nahi kiya hota (past result).' — 'If he weren't so lazy, he wouldn't have failed the exam last year.' Dekho, 'lazy hona' uska hamesha ka nature hai (present/general), but 'exam fail karna' ek specific past event hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (notebook me likhte hue) Achha, ab dono types clear ho gaye. Bas ek confusion hai — kaise pehchanenge ki konsa type use karna hai, jab exam me sentence diya ho?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Simple trick — dono clauses padho, aur socho har ek KIS TIME ki baat kar raha hai. Agar 'if' clause PAST ki baat kare aur result ABHI/PRESENT ka ho — Type A, structure hoga 'had/hadn't + V3' phir 'would/wouldn't + V1'. Agar 'if' clause PRESENT/GENERAL trait ki baat kare aur result PAST ka ho — Type B, structure hoga 'were/wasn't/general past-simple' phir 'would/wouldn't + have + V3'.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Achha ab practice karte hain — main ek Hindi sentence bolta hoon, tum bologi konsa type hai aur poora banao. 'Agar maine tumhare saath itna time nahi bitaya hota, aaj mujhe English itni achhi nahi aati.'

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (sochte hue) 'bitaya hota' — past. 'aati' — present. To ye Type A hoga — 'If I hadn't spent so much time with you, I wouldn't be so good at English now.'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (taali maarte hue) Perfect! Ab mera ek try karo — 'Agar wo itni ambitious nahi hoti (uska nature), usne wo bada scholarship apply hi nahi kiya hota.'

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: 'ambitious hona' — present/general nature. 'apply kiya hota' — past. To ye Type B hoga — 'If she weren't so ambitious, she wouldn't have applied for that big scholarship.'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (muskurate hue) Bilkul sahi! Dekho, hum dono ne mil ke ek tough topic bhi kitni asaani se seekh liya.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (halki si rahat ke saath muskurate hue) Sach kahoon, mujhe bhi lagta hai — agar us din tumne mera naam na diya hota, hum itni gehri baat kabhi na kar paate jitni ab kar paate hain.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (muskurate hue) To sab theek hai humare beech?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Sab theek hai, pagal. Chalo ab ghar chalte hain, kal se phir naya topic — poora mixed practice.

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'If I had studied medicine, I would have been a doctor now.'
quiz.answer: If I had studied medicine, I would be a doctor now.
quiz.explain: Mixed Conditional Type A (past cause, present result) me main clause 'would + V1' hota hai kyunki result abhi present me hai, 'would have' nahi (jo poora past ke liye hota hai).

quiz.q: Fill (Type A — past cause, present result): If she ___ (not/miss) that flight, she ___ (be) here with us today.
quiz.explain: 'If' clause past unreal (had + V3), main clause present result (would + V1) — classic Mixed Conditional Type A.

quiz.q: Which is a Mixed Conditional — a) If I had left earlier, I would have caught the train. b) If I hadn't taken that job, I wouldn't be living here now.
quiz.answer: b) If I hadn't taken that job, I wouldn't be living here now.
quiz.explain: Option b) me past ka decision (taking the job) ka effect abhi present me dikh raha hai ('wouldn't be living now') — ye hi Mixed Conditional ki pehchaan hai. Option a) pura Type 3 hai (dono clause past).

quiz.q: Identify the type: 'If he weren't so careless, he wouldn't have lost his phone yesterday.' Is 'if' clause about the present or past?
quiz.answer: Present/general trait ('he is careless' — his nature) causing a past result ('lost his phone yesterday') — this is Mixed Conditional Type B.
quiz.explain: 'Weren't careless' describes his general nature (present), while 'wouldn't have lost' describes a specific past event — the two time zones mix in the opposite direction from Type A.

quiz.q: Fill (Type B — present/general cause, past result): If she ___ (not/be) so kind, she ___ (not/help) that stranger yesterday.
quiz.explain: 'If' clause describes a general/present character trait ('weren't so kind'), and main clause describes a specific past action ('wouldn't have helped ... yesterday') — Type B pattern.

quiz.q: Translate to English (choose Type A or Type B correctly): 'Agar tum itne mehnati nahi hote (tumhara swabhav), tumhe wo scholarship pichle saal nahi milta.'
quiz.answer: If you weren't so hardworking, you wouldn't have got that scholarship last year.
quiz.explain: 'Mehnati hona' ek general trait hai (present), 'scholarship milna' ek specific past event hai — Type B ka pattern: If + were/general present, Subject + wouldn't have + V3.
